name: Infrastructure Foundation
scope_implemented: docker-compose.yml: postgres+timescale, redis, backend, dashboard-shell, worker(s), caddy on an internal network with only 443 exposed; live engine behind the 'live' profile (never started by default).
.env.example with safe placeholders (no real keys) + pydantic-settings config system with env validation (src/config/settings.py).
PostgreSQL schema + Alembic migration 0001 for jobs, job_logs, gates, gate_results, remediation_actions, approvals, audit_log.
Job orchestration skeleton (enqueue, progress, retry, cancel) with persisted job records + logs (src/jobs/*).
Per-service health-check endpoints + dashboard shell with authentication (src/api/app.py, src/api/auth.py).
Exchange-adapter skeleton + sync_exchange_metadata job stub; universe-builder skeleton (src/exchange, src/universe).
CLI 'make kill' independent of the dashboard process (src/killswitch.py, src/cli/main.py); full Makefile target set.
Gate scaffolding wired to configs/gates.yaml: INFRA, DB, QUEUE, STORAGE implemented; MON, BACKUP skeletons present.

Gate-runner CLI contract: 'make -s run-gate GATE=<id> FORMAT=json' and 'make -s run-all-gates FORMAT=json' emit a single GateResult JSON object to stdout matching configs/gate_result.schema.json exactly (top-level gate_id, overall, criteria[], report_path; each criterion uses id (not name) + status, optional failure_reason/remediation_steps); GATE/FORMAT are make variables, not flags.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: make docker-up && make health -> services green
acceptance_criteria.evidence: make health returns all components healthy (db/redis/storage/kill_switch); Docker available; live engine behind 'live' profile.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: make migrate idempotent; required tables exist
acceptance_criteria.evidence: alembic migration 0001; DB gate verifies required tables + indexes.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Job skeleton: enqueue/progress/retry/cancel; records + logs persisted
Dashboard shell requires auth; per-service health endpoints respond
make kill works with the dashboard stopped
Gates INFRA, DB, QUEUE, STORAGE return PASS; MON, BACKUP skeletons present
acceptance_criteria.evidence: make -s run-all-gates FORMAT=json; all four PASS, MON/BACKUP skeleton PASS.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: pytest, ruff, mypy green; no secrets committed; no live-mode default
acceptance_criteria.evidence: 36 passed; ruff clean; mypy no issues; .env gitignored; TRADING_MODE=PAPER default, live disabled.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Completion Report filled; assumptions in docs/decisions/

tests.coverage_risk_exec_adaptation: n/a (modules not implemented until later phases)
tests.mypy: ok (no issues found in 33 source files)

known_gaps_risks: Gate per-run JSON dumps under reports/gates/ are gitignored transient artifacts; report_path values above use a <ts> placeholder since the concrete timestamped file is regenerated each run.
MON and BACKUP are skeletons this phase (return PASS) and are hardened in later phases per the roadmap.
Downstream gates (DQ, UNIV, BT, ... LIVE) correctly report BLOCKED/NOT_RUN until their phases land.
next_step: Hand off to Reviewer Agent; do not begin Phase 2.
